top_stories.title: Microsoft to roll out Entra passkeys on Windows in late April
top_stories.summary: Microsoft is rolling out passkey support for phishing-resistant passwordless authentication to Microsoft Entra-protected resources from Windows devices starting late April. The update enables users to authenticate to Entra-protected resources using passkeys directly from Windows devices.

top_stories.title: Users advised to drop passwords and make room for passkeys
top_stories.summary: The UK's National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) is officially recommending that consumers replace passwords with passkeys, reversing decades of prior guidance. The agency states it will no longer recommend passwords where passkeys are available, citing passwords' weaker resistance to current cyber threats.

top_stories.title: 'Identity is the new battleground': Why your IT helpdesk is suddenly getting a lot of bizarre calls
top_stories.summary: Okta-related vishing attacks are targeting IT helpdesks through impersonation and social engineering tactics to manipulate support staff. Attackers exploit urgency and human trust to bypass authentication controls, enabling rapid escalation into large-scale data breaches.

top_stories.title: Phishing and MFA exploitation: Targeting the keys to the kingdom
top_stories.summary: A 2025 threat report identifies increasing attacker focus on exploiting weaknesses in MFA workflows and using compromised credentials to conduct phishing campaigns from trusted accounts. The attacks specifically target authentication trust mechanisms, with both MFA bypass techniques and credential-based phishing representing growing vectors against identity infrastructure.

top_stories.title: Vercel Employee's AI Tool Access Led to Data Breach
top_stories.summary: A Vercel employee's access to an AI tool resulted in a data breach via stolen OAuth tokens. Security researchers highlight OAuth tokens as an emerging primary attack vector for lateral movement in enterprise environments.

news.title: DORA and operational resilience: Credential management as a financial risk control
news.summary: The article examines how DORA's Article 9 mandates authentication and access control as legal obligations for EU financial entities. It details what the regulation requires for credential management and illustrates breach scenarios when those controls are absent.

news.title: Bridging the AI Agent Authority Gap: Continuous Observability as the Decision Engine
news.summary: The article addresses the governance and authorization challenges posed by AI agents in enterprise security, framing the issue as one of delegated authority rather than simply new actors. It focuses on identity delegation, observability, and decision-making frameworks relevant to how IAM professionals should govern AI agent access.

news.title: The New World ID: Proof of Human for the AI Era Scales Across the Digital Platforms People and Businesses Use Every Day
news.summary: World announced the next generation of World ID, a proof-of-human protocol, with Okta among the partners bringing the technology to businesses, AI agents, and consumers. The integration with Okta and other major platforms positions World ID as an identity verification layer aimed at distinguishing humans from AI agents in digital environments.

news.title: Bitwarden CLI npm package compromised to steal developer credentials
news.summary: The Bitwarden CLI npm package was compromised by attackers who uploaded a malicious @bitwarden/cli package containing a credential-stealing payload. The attack targeted developer credentials and had the potential to spread to other dependent projects through the npm supply chain.

news.title: UNC6692 Impersonates IT Help Desk via Microsoft Teams to Deploy SNOW Malware
news.summary: A threat actor group called UNC6692 is using Microsoft Teams impersonation of IT help desk personnel to deploy custom malware called SNOW on victim systems. The attack relies on social engineering via Teams chat invitations, a technique increasingly relevant to IAM defenders managing authentication and communication platform security.

news.title: Bitwarden CLI Compromised in Ongoing Checkmarx Supply Chain Campaign
news.summary: The Bitwarden CLI package (@bitwarden/cli@2026.4.0) was compromised as part of an ongoing Checkmarx supply chain campaign, with malicious code injected into a file called 'bw1.js,' according to findings from JFrog and Socket. Bitwarden is a widely used password manager, and its CLI tool is commonly used by IAM and security teams for credential and secrets management automation.

news.title: Vercel Finds More Compromised Accounts in Context.ai-Linked Breach
news.summary: Vercel disclosed that additional customer accounts were compromised in a security incident linked to Context.ai that allowed unauthorized access to its internal systems. The discovery came after Vercel expanded its investigation to include additional compromise indicators and reviewed requests to its network and environment.

news.title: Regular Password Resets Aren’t as Safe as You Think
news.summary: Specops Software has published research showing how regular password resets create security vulnerabilities by enabling helpdesk social engineering attacks. The research demonstrates how attackers can exploit password reset processes to achieve full account compromise by bypassing standard security controls.

news.title: Toxic Combinations: When Cross-App Permissions Stack into Risk
news.summary: Researchers disclosed that Moltbook, a social network for AI agents, exposed 35,000 email addresses and 1.5 million agent API tokens across 770,000 active agents via an unsecured database. The exposure included plaintext third-party credentials such as OpenAI API keys shared between agents in private messages, illustrating risks of cross-application permission stacking in agentic identity contexts.

news.title: Consumer Identity and Access Management Global Market Forecast Report 2026-2036, Profiles of Okta, Auth0, Ping Identity, ForgeRock, Microsoft, Salesforce, IBM, Amazon Web Services, Accenture
news.summary: A market forecast report projects the global Consumer Identity and Access Management (CIAM) market will grow from USD 12.6 billion in 2026 to USD 40.2 billion by 2036, representing a CAGR of 12.2%. The report covers key vendors including Okta, Auth0, Ping Identity, ForgeRock, Microsoft, Salesforce, IBM, AWS, and Accenture.

news.title: Non-Human Identity Access Management Market Global Forecast Report 2026-2036: Opportunities in Adoption of Zero Trust Architecture, Cloud-native Applications, and Regulatory Compliance Requirements
news.summary: A new market forecast report projects the global non-human identity (NHI) access management market will grow from $12.2 billion in 2026 to $38.8 billion by 2036, at a CAGR of 12.2%. The report covers market dynamics across regions and sectors, with growth drivers including zero trust architecture adoption, cloud-native applications, and regulatory compliance requirements.

news.title: No Exploit Needed: How Attackers Walk Through the Front Door via Identity-Based Attacks
news.summary: The article examines identity-based attacks as a primary breach vector, focusing on how attackers use stolen credentials, credential stuffing, and related techniques to gain unauthorized access. It addresses topics directly relevant to IAM professionals including authentication weaknesses and identity-centric threat patterns.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a senior member of the cybercrime group Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Buchanan admitted involvement in SMS phishing attacks in 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in the theft of tens of millions of dollars in cryptocurrency.

news.title: Making opportunistic cyberattacks harder by design
news.summary: Microsoft published details on how it secures Dynamics 365 and Power Platform through credential removal, attack surface reduction, and platform engineering practices. The approach focuses on eliminating standing credentials and reducing opportunities for opportunistic threats as part of Microsoft's secure-by-design initiative.

news.title: Securely connecting on-premises data systems to Amazon Redshift with IAM Roles Anywhere
news.summary: AWS published a technical guide on using IAM Roles Anywhere to establish secure, private connections between on-premises data systems and Amazon Redshift. The solution eliminates the need for long-lived access keys by leveraging certificate-based authentication to obtain temporary AWS IAM credentials.

news.title: Tycoon 2FA Phishers Scatter, Adopt Device Code Phishing
news.summary: Threat actors behind the Tycoon 2FA phishing-as-a-service platform have shifted tactics to adopt device code phishing attacks. The technique abuses legitimate OAuth device authorization flows to trick victims into granting account access, effectively bypassing MFA protections.

news.title: Two-Factor Authentication Breaks Free From the Desktop
news.summary: The article discusses extending two-factor authentication (2FA) beyond traditional desktop/IT environments to address physical world security threats. It covers implementing 2FA as an additional security barrier in non-traditional IT contexts where threat actors can bypass standard security systems.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence-linked hackers are exploiting vulnerabilities in older routers to mass harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens across more than 18,000 networks. The campaign operates without deploying malware, relying solely on router flaws to silently intercept authentication tokens at scale.
